description: Fix applyBrainPlan to fully reset route progress, persist brainPlan per project, and force a complete view re-render after analysis - so uploading a new project description properly clears old project state (especially route/cabling direction) and shows the fresh plan.
steps: Step 1 - Reset routeProgress in applyBrainPlan (qi-webapp/js/ui.js line ~3632): In the 'clear previously generated content' block at the top of applyBrainPlan, add `st0.routeProgress = {};` to completely wipe the old route progress data. Currently only `S.setRouteLaidKm(cab.id, 0)` is called later (which sets km to 0 but preserves old phase statuses from the previous project). The full wipe ensures no stale route data from the previous project persists.

Step 2 - Persist brainPlan per-project in the store (qi-webapp/js/store.js): Add a `brainPlan` field to the project state. In normalize(), ensure `s.brainPlan = s.brainPlan || null;`. Add two new API methods: `setBrainPlan(plan)` which sets `get().brainPlan = plan; save();` and `getBrainPlan()` which returns `get().brainPlan`. Export them on the Store API object.

Step 3 - Wire UI to persist/restore brainPlan (qi-webapp/js/ui.js): In runAnalyze (around line 3320), after `uiState.brainPlan = plan;`, also call `S.setBrainPlan(plan);`. On app load and project switch, restore: when the AFTER.brain function runs, check `S.getBrainPlan()` and if it exists, set `uiState.brainPlan = S.getBrainPlan();`. Also in the project-switch handler (line ~5865 `act === 'openproj'`), after `S.switchProject(id)`, restore `uiState.brainPlan = S.getBrainPlan() || null;`.

Step 4 - Force full view re-render after applyBrainPlan (qi-webapp/js/ui.js): At the end of applyBrainPlan (after the existing refreshHeader/refreshBadges calls), add `go(current);` to fully re-render the current view. This ensures Dashboard, Globe, Route Progress, or whatever view the user is on gets refreshed with the new data. The `current` variable is already in scope (module-level).

Step 5 - Clear uiState.brainPlan before overwriting in runAnalyze: Before `uiState.brainPlan = plan;`, add `uiState.brainPlan = null;` to ensure any stale reference is dropped before the new plan is set. This is a defensive measure.

Step 6 - Add new smoke tests (qi-webapp/smoke.js): Add assertions AFTER the existing brain tests (around line 755) to verify: (a) After running applyBrainPlan, routeProgress is freshly initialized (all phases 'Not started', laidKm 0), (b) Re-analyzing with a completely different description still results in clean route progress (no leftover from previous), (c) S.getBrainPlan() returns the applied plan (persistence), (d) After S.reset() + switchProject, uiState.brainPlan should be cleared.
acceptance_criteria: Running `npm test` passes all existing 783+ smoke assertions plus the new ones
After applyBrainPlan, routeProgress for all cable segments has all phases set to 'Not started' and laidKm = 0
Re-analyzing with a different project description completely resets route progress (no data from previous analysis persists)
uiState.brainPlan (and S.getBrainPlan()) stores the analysis result and survives view navigation
Switching projects restores the correct brainPlan for that project
The current view re-renders after analysis (go(current) is called)
verification: cd qi-webapp && npm run build:standalone && node verify-standalone.js
findings: Pre-existing bare FAIL in test.js (line 14, budget category test) exists on the base branch and is unrelated. The seedRouteEntry function in store.js sets cable phases based on cable.status (commissioned/in-progress), so applyBrainPlan now directly assigns fresh entries rather than using setRouteLaidKm to avoid re-seeding from cable status. The go(current) call is skipped when on the brain view to avoid wiping the preview DOM that renderBrainPreview just built.
